text: The odds ratio for hospitalization or emergency department visit was 0.30 (95% CI, 0.16 to 0.59; P<0.001) with bamlanivimab-etesevimab.
The odds ratio for virologic failure was 0.28 (95% CI, 0.11 to 0.74; P=0.006) with cabotegravir-rilpivirine.
The odds ratio for hospitalization or death was 0.29 (95% CI, 0.17 to 0.47; P<0.001) with casirivimab-imdevimab.
The odds ratio for heart failure was 0.62 (95% CI, 0.45 to 0.84; P=0.002) with intensive blood pressure control.
The odds ratio for myocardial infarction was 0.73 (95% CI, 0.65 to 0.82; P<0.001) with evolocumab.
The odds ratio for hospitalization for heart failure was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.52 to 0.87) with canagliflozin.
The odds ratio for hospitalization for heart failure was 0.65 (95% CI, 0.50 to 0.85; P=0.002) with empagliflozin.
The odds ratio for end-stage kidney disease was 0.68 (95% CI, 0.54 to 0.86; P=0.002) with canagliflozin.
The odds ratio for cardiovascular death or heart failure hospitalization was 0.83 (95% CI, 0.73 to 0.95; P=0.005) with dapagliflozin.
The odds ratio for the primary composite was 0.87 (95% CI, 0.75 to 1.01; P=0.06) with sacubitril-valsartan.
The odds ratio for cardiovascular death was 0.87 (95% CI, 0.76 to 1.00; P=0.048) with telmisartan.
The odds ratio for the primary outcome was 1.01 (95% CI, 0.94 to 1.09) comparing telmisartan to ramipril.
The odds ratio for major bleeding was 0.80 (95% CI, 0.69 to 0.93; P=0.003) with dabigatran 110mg.
The odds ratio for intracranial hemorrhage was 0.42 (95% CI, 0.30 to 0.58; P<0.001) with apixaban.
